name: Andouille in Comforting Barbecue Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Louisiana hot sauce OR 3 cups Ketchup
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the peanut oil in a large pot over medium heat.
2. Add onions and sauté until translucent or tender.
3. Add celery, bell pepper, and parsley; cook for 5 minutes.
4. Stir in garlic and cook for an additional minute.
5. Pour in steak sauce, hot sauce (or ketchup), cayenne pepper, and salt to taste.
6. Gently add Southern Comfort liquor.
7. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 2-3 hours until thickened.
8. Slice 1 lb of Andouille sausage into 1/4 inch slices.
9. Combine the sliced sausage with 1 cup of the sauce in a serving dish or chafing dish.
10. Heat the remaining sauce until hot, then serve over French bread or individual servings.
11. Serve with napkins and enjoy.
Makes about 3/4 gallon. Can be stored refrigerated for several weeks.
